risk_if_not_implemented: Without Cryptographic Module Authentication, sensitive data may be exposed to interception or unauthorized disclosure, resulting in confidentiality breaches.

compensating_control_1.name: Cryptographic Key Management
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate cryptographic key management controls to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of keys.
compensating_control_1.justification: Cryptographic Key Management (CRY-09) provides cryptographic protection that compensates for the absence of Cryptographic Module Authentication (IAC-12) by ensuring data confidentiality and integrity through alternative technical means. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Identification & Authentication for Organizational Users
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to uniquely identify and centrally Authenticate, Authorize and Audit (AAA) organizational users and processes acting on behalf of organizational users.
compensating_control_2.justification: Identification & Authentication for Organizational Users (IAC-02) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Cryptographic Module Authentication (IAC-12) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
